Perfect approach. Let's build your mental model first before touching any code. The Big Picture — What NoteMind Actually Is Think of NoteMind like a smart librarian. You give the librarian a bunch of textbooks (your PDFs). The librarian reads them, memorizes the important parts, and when you ask a question, they find the relevant pages and give you an answer. That's exactly what your app does — just with code instead of a person. The Two Halves of the App NoteMind ├── Frontend (what the user

es of the App NoteMind ├── Frontend (what the user sees — React) └── Backend (the brain — FastAPI + AI) Frontend is like the counter at the library — where you walk in, drop off books, and ask questions. Backend is the back room — where the actual reading, indexing, and answering happens. They talk to each other over HTTP — the frontend sends requests, the backend sends back responses. The Backend File System — The Story backend/ ├── app.py ← The front door ├── ingest.py ← The receiving desk

The front door ├── ingest.py ← The receiving desk ├── pdf_loader.py ← The reader ├── embeddings.py ← The memorizer ├── vector_store.py ← The filing cabinet ├── retrieval.py ← The searcher ├── llm.py ← The answerer └── data/ └── uploads/ ← The storage room Here's the story of what happens when a user uploads a PDF and asks a question: Story Part 1 — Uploading a Document User drops a PDF ↓ app.py ← receives the file from the browser ↓ ingest.py ← saves it to data/uploads/ ↓ pdf_loader.py ← opens

saves it to data/uploads/ ↓ pdf_loader.py ← opens the PDF , reads all the text, cuts it into small chunks ↓ embeddings.py ← converts each chunk into numbers (called vectors — this is how AI understands text) ↓ vector_store.py ← saves all those number-vectors into a FAISS index (a special searchable filing cabinet) Story Part 2 — Asking a Question User types a question ↓ app.py ← receives the question from the browser ↓ embeddings.py ← converts the question into numbers too ↓ retrieval.py ←

rts the question into numbers too ↓ retrieval.py ← searches the FAISS filing cabinet for chunks that are similar to the question ↓ llm.py ← takes those relevant chunks + the original question, sends them to an AI model, gets an answer back ↓ app.py ← sends the answer back to the browser The Frontend File System — The Story frontend/src/ ├── App.tsx ← The map (decides which page to show) ├── pages/ │ ├── Index.tsx ← Landing page │ ├── Dashboard.tsx ← Where you manage your documents │ ├──

hboard.tsx ← Where you manage your documents │ ├── Chat.tsx ← Where you ask questions │ ├── Flashcards.tsx ← Study mode │ └── Compare.tsx ← Compare two documents ├── components/ │ ├── ChatWindow.tsx ← The actual chat box │ ├── DocUploader.tsx ← The file upload box │ ├── AppSidebar.tsx ← The left navigation bar │ └── ui/ ← Reusable buttons, cards etc └── lib/ └── dummyData.ts ← Fake data used while backend isn't ready The frontend is like a building with multiple rooms. App.tsx is the hallway

ilding with multiple rooms. App.tsx is the hallway that decides which room to take you to based on the URL. How Frontend and Backend Connect Frontend (React) Backend (FastAPI) │ │ │ POST /api/upload ──────→│ app.py receives file │ │ │ POST /api/query ──────→│ app.py receives question │ │ │ ←────── JSON response ───│ sends answer back The frontend never touches the AI or files directly — it just sends HTTP requests to the backend and displays whatever comes back. whatever comes back. When you're ready, just say "let's start file by file" and we'll go through each one line by line starting with app.py — the front door of your backend.
